title: 1.1: Tests that custom metadata headers passed to stream constructor are received by the gRPC server
1.2: Tests that data event handlers receive messages when server sends multiple data messages before an error
1.3: Tests that error event handler fires when server returns a gRPC error status
1.4: Tests that data events and the error event are both recorded during the stream
1.5: Tests that server responses are received after client calls end() on the stream
1.6: Tests that end event handler is triggered when stream completes after receiving all server responses
1.7: Tests that timestamp metadata is provided to data event handler callbacks
1.8: Tests that timestamp metadata is provided to end event handler callbacks
1.9: Tests that timestamp value is validated as a reasonable Unix timestamp
1.10: Tests that custom tags passed in stream parameters appear in emitted metrics samples
1.11: Tests that custom tags are present in emitted metrics samples
2.1: Places the gRPC stream tests in the grpc module directory
3.1: All tests use Go testing framework with *testing.T parameter
3.2: All tests have descriptive function names starting with Test
3.3: All tests call t.Parallel() to enable parallel test execution
3.4: All tests use testify assert or require for assertions
3.5: All tests use the callRecorder pattern to capture and verify event sequences
4.1: Tests that require server behavior register a gRPC service stub
4.2: Tests properly initialize test state before executing JavaScript code
4.3: Tests with event handlers use an event loop mechanism for async operations
